steps.title: Introduce the appositive
steps.content: After "civil rights history," the phrase beginning "the former" explains the two events just named. It is an appositive phrase, so it needs a comma before it, not a new sentence or an added connector.

steps.title: Avoid extra structure
steps.content: A gives no punctuation before the appositive. B adds "as," which changes the structure, and D creates a sentence fragment after the period. A single comma before "the former" correctly attaches the explanation to the main clause. The answer is C.
